title: HMM ablation decision artifact
description: OUTPUT-ONLY schema for the final runs/_hmm_decisions.json artifact (plan docs/planning/temporal_optimization_plan.md §2.2 决策规则). Sister-file precedent: scripts/_r2_decision_schema.json. This schema is NOT used to validate raw per-cell metric inputs into apply_decision_rule — those are caught at dataclass construction (typed Python). The runner validates the SERIALIZED output against this schema BEFORE atomic-rename to runs/_hmm_decisions.json; any per-cell input failure that occurred upstream is already serialized into the decision='executor_error' notes field by the time this schema runs. Validation library: `jsonschema` (Draft-7), matching scripts/_r2_decision_schema.json's precedent.

deploy_via_tracker_artifact_exception.description: MUST be false unless decision == 'deploy' and the residual illegal-transition count was fully traceable to ByteTrack ID-switch / short-track noise. Validators should reject decision != 'deploy' with this flag true.

sensitivity_sweep.description: Plan §2.2 mandates exactly 3 rows over laplace_alpha ∈ {0.01, 0.1, 1.0}. The allOf+contains tuple guarantees one row per required alpha (combined with minItems/maxItems=3, this forces exactly one of each — Draft-7 has no minContains/maxContains). Exactly one row MUST set deploy_anchor=true; anchor-uniqueness remains a runner invariant since JSON Schema cannot express 'exactly one of array elements has property X = true' — b-stage runtime assertion required.

allOf.description: decision='defer' → reason ∈ {defer_flicker_band, defer_illegal_or, tracker_artifact_evidence_missing, tracker_artifact_evidence_malformed, tracker_artifact_evidence_unverifiable}.
decision='drop' → reason ∈ {drop_flicker_low, drop_illegal_weak, drop_catchall, tracker_artifact_evidence_missing, tracker_artifact_evidence_malformed, tracker_artifact_evidence_unverifiable}.

DecisionReason.description: Required reason code for every decision row (top-level + ModeCell). Pairs with truth-table rows R1a-R5 in apply_decision_rule docstring. Validators should reject decision='deploy' with reason ∉ {'deploy', 'deploy_via_tracker_artifact_exception'}, and decision='executor_error' with reason not starting 'executor_error_'.

baseline_total_transitions.description: Split from a single total_transitions field so denominator drift between baseline and candidate is grep-able / rejectable. The executor MUST assert baseline_total_transitions == candidate_total_transitions; mismatch surfaces as decision='executor_error'.

baseline_eligible_track_count.description: Split from a single eligible_track_count field, same hazard as the transitions denominator. Same eligible-tracks rule on the same EVAL JSONL must produce identical track-set membership regardless of smoother. Executor asserts baseline_eligible_track_count == candidate_eligible_track_count; mismatch surfaces as decision='executor_error'.

baseline_total_track_count.description: Split from a single total_track_count field. Executor asserts baseline_total_track_count == candidate_total_track_count; mismatch surfaces as decision='executor_error'.

map_no_regression.description: Derived: true iff total_map_ap_delta_pp >= -map_regression_tolerance_pp AND every safety_class_ap_deltas[i].passed == true. Validators MUST recompute and assert agreement; mismatch surfaces as executor_error_threshold_oob.

map_regression_tolerance_pp.description: v2.0 LOCK: pinned to 0.2 pp. Wider tolerances would let the AP regression gate become a no-op. Changing this value requires a fresh adversarial review pass.

tracker_artifact_evidence_ref.description: Required for the deploy exception clause. null OR object with all 5 fields non-empty (frame_start <= frame_end is a runner invariant — JSON Schema Draft-7 cannot express cross-property numeric inequality). Missing / null / malformed / unverifiable evidence pairs with tracker_artifact_residual=true cascades the row through deploy without raising executor_error; reason code distinguishes evidence_missing / malformed / unverifiable.

candidate_run_submitter.description: Identity of the engineer / pipeline that submitted the candidate run. Runner asserts != tracker_artifact_evidence_ref.reviewer for reviewer-independence (a reviewer cannot rubber-stamp their own submission).

track_trace_artifact_path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path to the track trace artifact (frame-by-frame ByteTrack output). Runner uses this to resolve trace_id → real track and to bound frame_start..frame_end.

track_trace_artifact_sha256.description: SHA256 of track_trace_artifact_path; pins the exact track set used for evidence verification.

eval_metrics_json_path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path to the eval-metrics JSON. Together with eval_metrics_json_sha256, the runner MUST recompute the file hash on disk and assert it matches; mismatch surfaces as decision='executor_error' with reason='executor_error_eval_metrics_hash_mismatch'.

eval_metrics_json_sha256.description: SHA256 of the eval-metrics artifact at eval_metrics_json_path. Runner verifies hash on disk before accepting metrics; stale / cherry-picked eval files fail this check.

eval_split_manifest_sha256.description: SHA256 of the frozen R2 val/audit manifest used to produce the eval JSON. Pins the data split; runner asserts equal to the round's frozen manifest hash.

baseline_detector_artifact_sha256.description: SHA256 of the baseline detector artifact (engine/.pt) the eval was run against. Pins the baseline; mismatch with the round's selected detector → executor_error_eval_metrics_hash_mismatch.

candidate_temporal_config_sha256.description: SHA256 of the candidate-side configs/temporal_hmm.yaml (or equivalent) that produced the candidate metrics. Pins HMM hyperparams (laplace_alpha, illegal_transition_policy, etc.); mismatch between two cells claiming the same alpha/mode is a runner-level invariant violation.

candidate_output_artifact_sha256.description: SHA256 of the candidate-side smoothed output artifact (track-level smoothed JSONL or equivalent). Together with track_trace_artifact_sha256 (baseline), pins both sides of the A/B comparison.

runner_revision_sha256.description: SHA256 of the runner script (or git-revision-derived hash) that produced this MetricsBlock. Two cells with the same alpha/mode but different runner_revision_sha256 are a runner-level invariant violation.

total_map_ap_delta_pp.description: Total mAP delta in percentage points (candidate - baseline). Deploy gate requires >= -map_regression_tolerance_pp.

passed.description: Derived: true iff full_val_support < 30 (excluded from gate) OR ap_delta_pp >= -map_regression_tolerance_pp.

safety_class_ap_deltas.description: Per-safety-class AP delta. Classes with full_val_support < 30 are excluded from the gate (passed=true regardless of ap_delta_pp).

ModeCell.description: Extended to mirror DecisionResult so non-anchor sweep cells (e.g. an alpha=0.01 viterbi cell that landed on 'drop') retain the diagnostic fields needed to audit why. Without these, a non-headline cell becomes 'drop' or 'executor_error' with no trail. The if/then conditional below enforces deploy_via_tracker_artifact_exception=false whenever decision is not 'deploy', closing the audit-corruption hole where a non-deploy row could carry the deploy-exception flag.
